items.summary: The prominent environmentalist declares the sand mine project "an industrial nightmare" after visiting the area.

items.title: Premier says Victoria Park protesters shouldn't 'get between bulldozers' as work begins
items.summary: The last protesters were removed from Victoria Park in the early hours of Monday morning.

items.title: Antisemitism royal commission rejects federal government's bid to keep cabinet documents confidential
items.summary: Commissioner Virginia Bell says the documents will provide a comparison of the resourcing given to counterterrorism agencies before and after August 2024, when the terror threat level was raised to "probable".

items.title: Large flock of critically endangered swift parrots seen near Port Macquarie
items.summary: The sighting of critically endangered parrots is seen as "really exciting" after hardly any were seen across NSW last year. But a bird expert warns the species still faces significant threats.

items.title: Mary Fowler's Manchester City wins FA Cup, beating Dario Vidošić's Brighton and Hove Albion
items.summary: The Women's FA Cup has a distinctly Australian flavour with Mary Fowler's Manchester City thumping Charlize Rule's Brighton and Hove Albion, coached by compatriot Dario Vidošić, whose father was honoured by fans at Wembley.

items.title: Joao Fonseca reaches first grand slam quarterfinal while Iga Świątek is out of French Open
items.summary: Nineteen-year-old Joao Fonseca beats two-time French Open runner-up Casper Ruud to reach his first grand slam quarterfinal, while four-time champion Iga Świątek suffers her earliest exit since 2019.

items.title: Defence Minister says Australia could still buy five US submarines under AUKUS
items.summary: Defence Minister Richard Marles has defended a shake-up to AUKUS that would involve Australia buying three second-hand Virginia-class submarines from the United States.

items.title: Australia scraps new Virginia-class submarines in AUKUS shakeup
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: WA storms leave thousands without power as wild weekend weather continues
items.summary: Tens of thousands of homes are left without power and the SES receives hundreds of calls for help, with the roofs torn off two apartment buildings, as WA's southern half is hammered by more severe weather.

items.title: Swifts Creek Bush Nursing Centre after-hours nursing cuts worry residents
items.summary: Swifts Creek is an hour-and-a-half drive from the nearest hospital and residents are worried cuts to after-hours care at its Bush Nursing Centre might leave the community at risk.

items.title: Cattle company inadvertently tipped off Queensland DPI to biosecurity breach
items.summary: A NSW company tipped off authorities to the illegal movement of its cattle, triggering the investigation that led to the largest-ever biosecurity fine under a Queensland law.

items.title: US Immigration and Customs Enforcement sued over alleged inhumane conditions at detention camp
items.summary: Civil rights groups allege people at the largest immigration detention centre in Texas are being beaten, abused and denied medical care at the camp.

items.title: Chalmers accuses One Nation, Coalition of being 'three-ring circus' — as it happened
items.summary: Treasurer Jim Chalmers has accused One Nation and the Coalition of seeking to "benefit" from people's "legitimate concerns" about the economy.

items.title: Concerns native forests could be part of Cement Australia's 'sustainable' fuel option as it moves away from coal
items.summary: Used tyres and wood waste will soon help to power a large cement manufacturing site in Tasmania as Cement Australia moves away from coal to alternative fuels, but some remain concerned the environmental benefit may be offset if the wood waste is sourced from logged native forests.

items.title: Natural hydrogen potential like 'tripping over gold', SA minister says
items.summary: It's been about for a century, but companies are springing to action to find "gold hydrogen" underground around the country, as Australia and the world strive to reach net zero emissions by 2050.

items.title: Socceroos reveal 26-player squad for 2026 FIFA World Cup
items.summary: Recently realigned Cristian Volpato and Japan-born Tete Yengi are in line for potential national debuts after being named in Australia's squad for this year's World Cup.

items.title: Iran weekly briefing: Trump demands changes to peace plan as Israel pushes ahead
items.summary: Donald Trump is reported to be amending a peace plan, which could fuel further delays in finding a final agreement.

items.title: Women reveal Byron Bay's predatory 2000s culture of rape, humiliation and shame
items.summary: Rachel Kila has waived her right to anonymity to join dozens of others speaking out about the experiences of young girls in Byron Bay in the early 2000s, when they say it became normalised for older boys and men to groom and have sex with girls, then publicly humiliate them.

items.title: Melbourne Fringe board rejects artists' request for 'fair cut' of festival takings
items.summary: An open letter to festival organisers that attracted almost 600 signatures asked them to "give artists a fair cut", with specific requests to change the way fees are calculated.

items.title: Italian and Brazilian authorities investigate possible Ebola cases as outbreak grows
items.summary: The true reach of the Ebola outbreak in central Africa could be much wider than the official figures published by central African authorities, the World Health Organization is warning.

items.title: Is this the tax reform hill Albanese and Chalmers want to die on?
items.summary: Why has Labor decided to spend the political capital of a large majority on a minor change to capital gains tax on businesses?

items.title: Liberal Party quietly publishes paper grappling with party's failure to attract women, young people
items.summary: The Liberal Party has quietly published a paper that floats offering a cut price $10 digital membership to join the party, in an effort to offset its declining numbers.

items.title: National home values flatline in May for the first time since January 2025

items.title: How Trials grew up, got sober and confronted his past on debut solo album Hendle
items.summary: The prolific A.B. Original rapper-producer talks about learning to "marry the wrong feeling with the right music".

items.title: Myanmar blast kills dozens in rebel-held village, mining explosives blamed
items.summary: Authorities say the blast was likely caused by explosives used for mining that had been stored in a warehouse.

items.title: Family lawyers say there's growing interest in 'prenups'. Here's how they work

items.title: Ian Thorpe on learning to fit in after a career spent standing out
items.summary: Ian Thorpe became a global star as a teenager, attracting both opportunities and scrutiny. Now, he wishes he'd done some things differently.

items.title: This week in history quiz, June 1: Quiz: Will you be crowned the history champ?
items.summary: Who took the first five-wicket haul in one-day cricket? And which state capital was the last to get ABC TV?

items.title: Young Australians reject budget and One Nation popularity climbs further in new poll
items.summary: Labor's federal budget is falling flat with its target demographic as millennials say they would rather vote for One Nation than Labor or the Greens, according to a new Redbridge poll.

items.title: Horses bolt through streets of Rome after police officer lights unauthorised fireworks
items.summary: Fireworks set off without permission by a police officer during a rehearsal for Italy's Republic Day parade startled dozens of horses, sending them bolting through the capital and injuring several riders.

items.title: Photos emerge of rescued Laos divers smiling and in good spirits as search continues for two others
items.summary: The remaining four men from a group of five were freed from the flooded cave on Saturday evening, in extraordinary and emotional scenes, as search efforts continue for two people thought to still be trapped inside.

items.title: West Coast claims fourth win as Essendon's injury nightmare continues
items.summary: West Coast makes light work of Essendon at Perth Stadium, as the Bombers lose another promising youngster to a serious injury.

items.title: As Iran's internet comes online after months-long blackout, first posts send anti-regime videos viral
items.summary: After being cut off from the world for more than 12 weeks, Iranians have come back online. Their initial reactions are joy, shock and confusion.

items.title: Why are e-bikes suddenly all over Australia's streets?
items.summary: When Barnaby Joyce was deputy prime minister and transport minister in 2021, his assistant minister Kevin Hogan made a quiet, but hugely significant change to the Road Vehicle Standards Act.

items.title: Haas laments Broncos' BS as Maguire warns against hoping for another miracle
items.summary: After four straight losses, the 5-7 Broncos are in the same position as last year before launching an audacious run to the premiership, but coach Michael Maguire says the team should not expect to be able to do that again.

items.title: The spy network that fed Castro intel ahead of plane downing at heart of murder charges
items.summary: In the indictment of former Cuban president Raúl Castro, prosecutors alluded to a network of spies called The Wasp Network. Here is what happened when they were deployed to Florida in the 1990s.

items.title: US fires missile into commercial ship trying to reach Iran, Central Command says
items.summary: The US military fires a missile into the engine room of the merchant vessel trying to break through its blockade of Iranian ports, the Central Command says.

items.title: Man taken to hospital after tree falls on car, as WA prepares for more wild weather
items.summary: Two people have had a lucky escape after a tree fell onto their car on a major highway in WA's South West, as strong winds lashed the region.

items.title: Love story: Locking down the 'one that got away'
items.summary: For a long time, I considered my teenage crush the one that got away. That all changed when we reconnected two decades later.

items.title: Hundreds arrested in Paris after violent clashes as Paris Saint-Germain wins Champions League
items.summary: More than 50 police officers are wounded, with most suffering minor injuries, as football fans set off fires and vandalise shops, with one small group even trying to storm a Paris police station.

items.title: Donald Trump to headline Great American State Fair for 250th anniversary after artists drop out
items.summary: Martina McBride, Bret Michaels, Young MC, Morris Day and The Time and The Commodores all said they would not perform after alleging they were misled about the nature of the event.

items.title: Australian Dance Theatre premieres RISE at Bundanon Art Museum
items.summary: The Australian Dance Theatre's first Indigenous artistic director had some dark days after the Voice referendum, but he shines a light on reconciliation in a new outdoor performance set to the rhythm of the rising Sun.

items.title: Socceroos defeated by Mexico despite encouraging second-half showing
items.summary: The Socceroos are beaten 1-0 by Mexico in their penultimate World Cup warm up match despite an improved and impressive second-half performance at the Rose Bowl.

items.title: Police find autistic boy who went missing in wild weather in Perth overnight
items.summary: An 11-year-old autistic boy, who is non-verbal, has been found after going missing from Perth's western suburbs in wild weather on Saturday afternoon.

items.title: Budget won't be 'main driver' if house prices fall, housing minister says
items.summary: The housing minister says the federal budget will have only a "mild" impact on house prices, and if predictions bear out that house prices may drop by 10 per cent, that won't be due to the government's tax changes.

items.title: Australia loses first Pakistan ODI, undone by debutant spinner Arafat Minhas
items.summary: Australia drops the first game in its limited-overs series in Pakistan as spinner Arafat Minhas takes 5-32 in his maiden one-day international.

items.title: Madeleine Ogilvie's downfall started with a seemingly innocuous question, and the end came quickly
items.summary: It started with a seemingly innocuous question. Over the course of several months, Madeleine Ogilvie had many opportunities to provide clarification, but didn't until last week. Then the end came quickly.

items.title: Push to protect family homes of MPs amid uptick in threats of political violence
items.summary: As threats against elected officials grow, so too is a question in the mind of many politicians — should the public pay for home security for our elected representatives?

items.title: Family gifts historical collection of images captured by pioneering WA photographer Bert Saw

items.title: NT Writers Festival launches story of Timorese asylum seekers who took fishing boat to Australia
items.summary: Jose da Costa's escape from Indonesian-occupied East Timor in 1995 has been detailed in a new book launched at the Northern Territory Writers Festival this weekend.

items.title: Mr Skelly animations tackling human themes of death, loss winning over fans worldwide

items.title: Lights at Canberra's Manuka Oval lean inwards thanks to strict planning rule
items.summary: If the six light poles skirting Manuka Oval look like they're on a lean, it's because they are. The 47-metre-high poles slant inwards because otherwise they couldn't be approved under an unusual Canberra planning rule.

items.title: Anniversary of national parks' return to traditional owners celebrated on NSW South Coast

items.title: Daria Kasatkina beaten by Aryna Sabalenka, Coco Gauff's French Open defence ended by Anastasia Potapova
items.summary: Aryna Sabalenka ends Australia's campaign at the French Open with a straightforward win over Daria Kasatkina, while Coco Gauff joins the slew of big names knocked out early in Paris.

items.title: University students bioremediate Agent Orange chemicals manufactured for Vietnam War from Sydney waterways
items.summary: More than 50 years after the Vietnam War, students are working to remove Agent Orange remnants from a waterway connected to Sydney Harbour, where the chemicals were manufactured.

items.title: Whitsundays artist creates fish leather from seafood waste
items.summary: In the front yard of her home in the picturesque Whitsundays, Felicity Chapman strips the last pieces of flesh and sinew from a strip of barramundi skin to make wearable leather art.

items.title: Paris Saint-Germain beats Arsenal on penalties in Champions League final
items.summary: Paris Saint-Germain beats Arsenal in a tense shootout in the Champions League final to be crowned European champions for the second straight year.

items.title: As New Zealand cracks down on gang members, one pastor is trying to heal them
items.summary: In a country where there now more gang members than police officers, a former gang member turned Christian pastor is trying to turn things around for others by addressing the root causes of gang life.

items.title: Life and death in the outback: Why Australians are still dying in the bush
items.summary: Kelly’s son and ex-husband died "stupid" deaths in the Australian outback. She’s on a mission to make sure no more lives are lost.

items.title: History-making mayor of Maribyrnong, Mohamed Semra, is only getting started
items.summary: At just 27, Mohamed Semra is not your average mayor. He overcame a childhood stutter and a viral moment in a Melbourne Apple Store to make political history – and he is just getting started.

items.title: Nearly 80 years after Bitter Springs film, its Aboriginal actors are finally being understood
items.summary: The "huge swathes" of Pitjantjatjara lines in the 1949 film Bitter Springs are at last translated. Warning: This story contains images of Indigenous people who have died.

items.title: French Open men's draw back to glorious chaos with no clear favourite
items.summary: All the key favourites are now out of the men's draw at the French Open, but then again, this tournament has always been just a little bit different.

items.title: Periods prove a big barrier for disabled women's participation in sport
items.summary: Vanessa was forced to regularly skip sport because of her period. New research shows that more than 60 per cent of disabled athletes have done the same.

items.title: Mothers fear children's chronic illnesses are linked to bushfire smoke during pregnancy
items.summary: Years after bushfires covered their cities in smoke, mothers are left wondering if their children's chronic illnesses began with what was in the air before they were born.
